pr.title: Link multiple files, remove invalid files from configure.py, fix function call
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Open PR that updates build/link configuration by marking several previously NonMatching objects as Matching, removes invalid/duplicate object entries from configure.py, adjusts symbol metadata/names in GALE01 symbols.txt to use local compiler-style labels for newly linked data, and fixes WStar spawned logic to call it_80294624 instead of it_80294364. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the provided slice, so rationale is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: In configure.py, melee/pl/plattack.c, melee/pl/plbonus.c, and melee/ft/ftchangeparam.c were changed from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...), causing them to link as matching source.
In configure.py, invalid object entries for melee/ft/ft_3C61.c and melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c were removed.

In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, several data, sdata2, and sbss symbols were renamed or retyped from generic global lbl_* / ftCo_* names to local compiler-style @NNN symbols, including string constants, float/double constants, and jump tables around 0x803BCF20, 0x803C6F78, 0x804D7F20, and 0x804D8F60.
In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, lbl_804D6480 was changed to unk_804D6480 and its size was reduced from 0x8 to 0x2, preserving data:2byte metadata.
In src/melee/it/items/itwstar.c, include ordering was adjusted without changing the included headers.
In itWStar_Logic29_Spawned, the final function call was corrected from it_80294364(gobj) to it_80294624(gobj).
smart_moves: Updated symbols.txt in tandem with enabling additional Matching objects, which is important because newly linked source often exposes local constants, strings, jump tables, and scope/size details that must agree with the linker map.
Converted anonymous-looking global lbl_* constants to scoped local @NNN symbols where the diff shows they are local compiler-generated data, reducing unnecessary global symbol pollution.
Removed invalid configure.py entries rather than leaving stale NonMatching placeholders, avoiding attempts to build source paths that should not be present.
Fixed a wrong item function call with a minimal one-line change in itWStar_Logic29_Spawned, keeping the behavioral fix isolated from the larger linking/configuration updates.
decomp_lessons: When promoting a file from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, verify and update the corresponding GALE01 symbol names, local/global scope, object sizes, alignment, and data classifications.
Compiler-generated local constants in symbols.txt may need @NNN naming rather than lbl_* naming once their owning object is linked from C.
String/data blobs previously represented as a single lbl_* object can split into multiple local @NNN objects when better symbol boundaries are known.
Removing invalid object paths from configure.py is part of link hygiene; stale entries can hide build/link problems even if they are marked NonMatching.
Small item behavior fixes should be made directly at the call site when the diff evidence identifies a mistaken function target.
naming_conventions: Local compiler-generated data symbols use @NNN format, for example @187, @1035, @288, @1037, @229, and @232.
Generic address labels use lbl_XXXXXXXX, but this PR replaces several with scoped local @NNN symbols where evidence indicates local data.
Unknown data can use unk_XXXXXXXX, as shown by lbl_804D6480 becoming unk_804D6480.
Existing project object naming conventions remain visible in configure.py, such as plattack.c, plbonus.c, ftchangeparam.c, ftCo_* fighter files, and ftPp_* Popo files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote verified source files by changing Object(NonMatching, path) to Object(Matching, path) in configure.py.
Synchronize symbol table changes with matching promotions so local constants and jump tables have correct scope and sizes.
Use local @NNN labels for compiler-emitted strings, floats, doubles, and jump tables rather than exporting them as global lbl_* symbols.
Delete invalid object entries from the build list instead of attempting to keep them as NonMatching stubs.
Correct function target mismatches in source when a call goes to the wrong nearby item function address.
follow_up_queries: Check CI/build results for PR 2593 to confirm the newly promoted Matching objects link successfully.
Review whether the symbols.txt scope and size changes around 0x803BCF20, 0x803C6F78, 0x804D6480, 0x804D7F20, and 0x804D8F60 match the generated map.
Verify why itWStar_Logic29_Spawned should call it_80294624 rather than it_80294364, since no PR body or review comments explain the behavioral rationale.
Confirm that removed configure.py paths melee/ft/ft_3C61.c and melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.c are truly invalid and not renamed/moved files needing replacement entries.
